title: mfa — HTTP middleware
description: Two middleware. Both operate on the caller's authenticated session (post-Sanctum-resolution) and inspect the Identity's MFA posture + the session's step-up state. Neither is auto-applied globally — they mount on specific routes / actions via the #[UsesMiddleware] attribute on the action class OR via the `mfa.*` alias in route definitions.

middleware.description: Enforces that the caller has completed an MFA challenge IN THE CURRENT SESSION when their resolved Identity is one that has MFA enrolled AND holds a role in `config('mfa.required_for_roles')`. Rejects with 403 `MFA_CHALLENGE_REQUIRED` when the session's `mfa_challenge_completed_at` is missing OR older than the session's TTL. The rejection payload includes the initiation URL for a step-up challenge (POST /api/v1/auth/mfa/challenge/initiate) so the SPA can transparently prompt for the second factor. This closes the 'session cookie with expired MFA state' gap.
consumed_by: Every tenant-audience route where the caller's Identity has MFA enrolled AND holds a required-for-roles role — enforced globally on the /api/v1/* group by identity/auth's route configuration once the identity is resolved.
flow: 1. Resolve Identity from the Sanctum PAT (falls to identity/auth's ResolveIdentity middleware which runs at priority 25).
2. Check config('mfa.required_for_roles') against the User's roles (via the auth::HasRoles trait). If none match, PASS.

3. Check `Identity.mfa_enabled` (accessor). If false AND the User is inside the grace_days_after_enrollment window, PASS. If false AND outside the grace window, REJECT with MFA_ENROLMENT_REQUIRED (redirect to /me/mfa/totp/enroll/preview).
4. Read the session's `mfa_challenge_completed_at`. If null OR older than the session-TTL boundary, REJECT with MFA_CHALLENGE_REQUIRED + include the initiation URL.

middleware.description: Enforces that the caller completed an MFA challenge WITHIN THE LAST `config('mfa.step_up.sensitive_action_ttl_minutes')` (default 5). Used for sensitive-write actions (password change, MFA disable, recovery-code regen, credential delete). Rejects with 403 `MFA_CHALLENGE_TOO_STALE` and includes a step-up-challenge URL. The middleware runs AFTER `mfa.require_challenge_complete` (which handles the session-level enforcement) — same-session recentness is a tighter check.
flow: 1. Resolve Identity from the Sanctum PAT.
2. Read `Identity.mfa_enabled`. If false, PASS (no MFA to challenge against — the recent-challenge concept is undefined).
3. Read the session's `last_mfa_challenge_at`. If null OR `now() - last_mfa_challenge_at > step_up.sensitive_action_ttl_minutes`, REJECT with MFA_CHALLENGE_TOO_STALE.

consumed.notes: Not consumed directly — but the challenge-initiation endpoint (POST /auth/mfa/challenge/initiate) IS protected by this rate limiter, which is why a repeated step-up prompt cannot be used as a login-brute-force vector.
Priority 25. Runs before both mfa middleware so they see the resolved Identity.

body.message: Multi-factor authentication is required to continue.
body.hint: POST to initiate_url to receive a challenge_id, then submit the code to POST /api/v1/me/mfa/challenge/{challenge_id}/verify.

body.message: Please verify your identity to continue.
body.action_id: the #[UsesTrait(HasMfa)] action ID the caller was trying to invoke
body.hint: Complete a fresh challenge and re-submit the original request within :ttl_minutes minutes.

session_state_note: The session stores two MFA-related timestamps: `mfa_challenge_completed_at` (any successful challenge in the current session) and `last_mfa_challenge_at` (the MOST RECENT successful challenge, used by `mfa.require_recent_challenge`). Both are set atomically by identity/auth's `VerifyMfaChallengeAction` on success. Session revocation (e.g. via identity's `PasswordChanged` listener) implicitly clears both because the session itself is destroyed.
